Nasz konserwatyzm nie oznacza zamknięcia: Our conservatism doesn't mean we're closed to change. It means respect for code that works and makes money. Before we say 'rewrite', we ask: 'what if we can fix it?'.

Usprawniamy. Nie wynajdujemy koła od now: We improve. We don't reinvent the wheel.

Dług techniczny nie powstaje dlatego, że: Technical debt isn't born from bad code. It's born from lack of time, budget, and team to keep things tidy.
Nie stać Cię na miesięczny przestój i pr: Can't afford a month-long rewrite downtime? Neither can we. That's why we do it in small, safe pieces. Tests for the critical function today. Framework version bump tomorrow. CI/CD next week.
Krok po kroku, bez zatrzymywania produkc: Step by step, without stopping production, without risk.

Poniższe technologie to nie lista \"co z: The following technologies aren't a 'what we know' list. They're ecosystems we've worked in for years. Each evolved — we know how because we were there.
Czy AI poradzi sobie samo? Możliwe. Ale: Can AI handle it alone? Maybe. But there are a few buts.
Demokratyzuje dostęp do technologii. Kto: It democratizes access to tech. Someone who didn't know how to write 'hello world' a year ago now generates functions and writes tests. That's cool.
But there are a few buts.
Jeśli uważasz, że AI napisze Ci kompletn: If you think AI will write your complete app and you can fire your devs — we're asking not to argue, but to share the truth we learn from every code built this way.
Context window to nie to samo co zrozumi: Context window isn't the same as understanding architecture
AI ma limit. 100k tokenów? 200k? Jeśli T: AI has limits. 100k tokens? 200k? If your app has 50k lines of code, a DB with 300 tables, and business logic living in cache and 'stuff everyone knows' — AI doesn't know. It doesn't have context.
Człowiek siedzi w tym kodzie 2 lata i wi: A person sits in this code 2 years and knows why GET /users returns only active entries from the last 30 days. They know it without reading code. They know it because they talked to the product owner, remember the incident when forgetting that filter broke everything.
AI przeczyta funkcję, ale nie ma tej pam: AI reads the function but doesn't have that memory. Forgets every time.
AI ci wygeneruje kod, który będzie wyglą: AI will generate code that looks right. Has correct syntax. Compiles. Does the wrong thing.
Klasyk: AI generuje SQL, który logicznie: Classic: AI generates SQL that looks logically fine, but on production with a million rows it hangs. Or returns sorted wrong in 0.01% of cases — and your clients discover it six months later.
Osoba co pisze kod od 10 lat patrzy na t: Someone writing code 10 years looks at that SQL and sees: 'wait, but if Category changes after that INSERT...'. AI doesn't. AI writes code that passes 99.99% of tests.
Testów AI nie da się pisać lepiej niż ko: AI can't write tests better than code
AI pisze testy do kodu, który sam napisa: AI writes tests for code it wrote. Like asking someone to check their own homework.
Testy muszą być napisane przez kogoś, kt: Tests must be written by someone who: (1) knows what edge cases can happen, (2) knows this project's bug history, (3) knows what business sees as catastrophe.
AI tego nie wie. Napisze testy na \"happ: AI doesn't know. Writes tests for the happy path. Thinks that's enough.
Jeśli AI wygeneruje kod, który spowoduje: If AI-generated code causes a data leak or costs clients money — AI won't sit in front of regulators.
Ty będziesz. Albo Twój zespół. Albo Twoj: You will. Or your team. Or your company.
AI to narzędzie. Narzędzie nie podpisuje: AI is a tool. Tools don't sign contracts. Don't take responsibility. Don't say 'sorry, we should have checked better'.
Wdrożyć to nie to samo co napisać: Deploying isn't the same as writing

Monitoring — co patrzysz, żeby wiedzieć,: Monitoring — what you watch to know something's breaking
I 20 rzeczy więcej, które AI wygeneruje: And 20 more things AI glosses over that you'll find missing at 3am when the system's down
AI to jest cheap (albo free, jeśli wycią: AI is cheap (or free if you hack the API). But when something goes wrong, you'll want solutions, and that costs.
Senior developer co potrzebuje 2 dni na: Senior dev who needs 2 days to diagnose? $500-1000. But you're done. AI solo? Wake up with a $2500 problem you'll debug 3 weeks.
OK, ale co gdy masz AI + dobrą osobę?: OK, but what if you have AI + good person?
Osoba pisze architekturę. Pisze brudnopi: Person writes architecture. Writes DB sketch. Documents what should happen. Then AI writes functions faster. Person reads, tests, asks 'what if...'. AI generates variants.
Osoba pisze testy pod edge case-y. AI pí: Person writes edge-case tests. AI writes the rest. Person handles deployment — AI writes scripts. Person does performance testing — AI analyzes results.
W tej kombinacji: AI robi 70% pracy. Oso: In this combo: AI does 70% work. Person does 30%, but 30% is decisions. Decisions are what actually matter.
Czemu my nie robimy tego całym AI?: Why don't we do it all with AI?
Bo nas pytają. Klienci przychodzą i mówi: Because clients ask us. They show up saying: 'I generated code 6 months, spent $50 on API calls, but the system's unreadable, nobody knows how to change it, and half the features are buggy'.
My wiemy co robić. Znamy legacy. Znamy e: We know what to do. We know legacy. We know edge cases. We know what happens when the DB has a million rows. We know how to change it without taking the server down.
I tak, używamy AI. Do generowania testów: Yeah, we use AI. For test generation. Code analysis. Documentation. But AI doesn't decide. People do.
Ale \"najfajniejsza zabawka\" to nie to: But 'cool tech' isn't the same as 'complete solution'.
Jeśli chcesz zmienić architekturę — musi: If you want to change architecture — you need someone who knows architectures.
Jeśli chcesz wdrożyć bez psucia bazy — m: If you want to deploy without breaking the DB — you need someone who knows migrations.
Jeśli chcesz żeby kod żył 5 lat i nikt n: If you want code that lives 5 years and nobody loses their mind reading it — you need someone who knows what that means.
